_comment: Calibration for farm ranking. Refitted from the player's own run log once enough clean
full clears accumulate; these seeds come from 253 recorded full clears across 10 stages.
A full clear means mobsKilled == totalMobs, so the measured damage covers the whole stage.
data/engine/farm_stages.json totalHP is TEN TIMES the real stage HP. Divide by this.
Evidence: tableHP / measuredDamage over 10 stages spanning 560 -> 2,073,809 table HP
(a 3700x range) lands in 9.79-10.70, and the fitted single scalar leaves every stage
within 6%. The ratio does NOT track monster count (count=10 gives 10.00, count=153
gives 10.70), which rules out the alternative explanation that the meter under-counts
damage per monster. Independently corroborated by live memory: monsters on stage 1208
read HP_MAX ~710 while the table implies ~6,256 each.
Consuming totalHP raw is what makes the reference app's farm ranking wrong.
clearSec = perWave*waves + perMonster*monsters + hp/dps, with hp already descaled.
Fitted on per-stage medians so the 224 runs on stage 1206 cannot dominate the fit.
Mean absolute relative error 4.2%, worst stage 12.2%, over clear times of 41s-283s.
The two-parameter form (waves + hp only) that the reference app uses cannot fit both
ends: it lands at 14.5% mean error and overpredicts stage 1101 by 70%, because small
stages are gated by spawn pacing rather than by damage.
CAVEAT (see movementSpeedConfound below): perWaveSec was fitted at a near-constant party
movement speed (~10.38 across all 253 source runs). It is now confirmed to be partly a
movement-speed-dependent travel term, not a true constant. Treat it as valid ONLY near
MS~10.4 until movementSpeedConfound's decomposition is calibrated with real proportions.
fittedDpsNote: This fitted DPS is roughly 1.8x the auto-attack DPS the engine models for the same
party (~715). The gap is skill damage, which is not yet modelled (M1.5). Prefer this
fitted value over the modelled one for clear-time prediction until skills land.

_comment: User's own hypothesis, tested live: is the per-wave time actually a movement/travel term
in disguise? All 253 seed runs above were logged at essentially one movement speed
(party avg 10.38, min 9.51 across 254 runs, 0.0% variation) so the flat perWaveSec fit
COULD NOT distinguish 'true constant' from 'distance / speed at speed=10.4'.
Direct live test on stage 1301: sampled MonsterSpawnManager's alive-monster list + summed
HP every ~20-200ms for 210s (avg MS 10.38, boots equipped) and again for 260s after the
player removed all boots (avg MS 9.89, -4.7%; slowest hero -5.0%). Classified time into
'0 monsters alive' (waiting for the next spawn -- should be speed-independent) vs 'monsters
alive but no HP dropped for >1.2s' (closing distance before the party can hit anything --
should scale as 1/speed).
RESULT:
spawn-wait segments : 0.96s -> 0.88s  (flat within noise; NOT speed-dependent, as expected)
travel segments      : 2.28s -> 2.54s  (+11.4%) while MS dropped 4.7%
Party DPS was ~unchanged across the two sessions (118->122, 158->161, 423->431), so the
travel-segment increase is not an artifact of the party also hitting softer.
CONCLUSION: confirmed directionally -- part of clear time IS a movement-speed-dependent
travel term, distinct from the fixed spawn-wait timer. perWaveSec above is NOT a pure
constant; it silently bakes in MS~10.4 and will read wrong for any party whose movement
speed differs meaningfully from that.
NOT YET RESOLVED: the exact scaling law. A naive distance/speed model predicts +4.9% for a
-4.7% speed drop; the observed +11.4% is over 2x that. Monster spawn POSITIONS are random
per session -- these two recordings are not a controlled repeat of the same path, so some
of the gap is spawn-layout variance rather than a real speed-scaling law. One before/after
pair cannot separate the two. Effective-distance check (travelSec * avgMS, which should be
a constant under a pure d/v model): 23.67 before vs 25.11 after -- a 6% mismatch, i.e. real
but modest disagreement, consistent with 'confirmed effect, imprecise magnitude'.
TODO before trusting a fitted travel constant: repeat this instrumented sampling across
several more movement-speed levels (multiple runs per level, same stage) so spawn-position
noise averages out. Until then, farm ranking should either (a) hold perWaveSec fixed and
flag rankings as calibrated near MS~10.4 only, or (b) apply a directionally-correct but
provisional 1/speed correction and label its magnitude as unverified -- never present a
point estimate here as validated the way stageHpScale above is.
